instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200 degrees C). Lightly spray a 12-cup standard muffin tin with cooking spray.
Crack 1 egg into each muffin cup, then stir in 2 tablespoons Cheddar cheese, 2 tablespoons ham, and 1 1/2 tablespoons milk.
Bake in the preheated oven until set and a nice golden yellow color, about 15 minutes.

holiday-turkey-brine.name: Holiday Turkey Brine
instructions: Combine vegetable broth, sea salt, brown sugar, peppercorns, rosemary, sage, and thyme in a large stockpot. Bring to a boil, stirring frequently until salt and sugar dissolve. Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature.
Pour the water, broth mixture, and apple cider vinegar into a 5-gallon bucket; stir until brine is combined. Add the apples, onion, Worcestershire sauce, and garlic to the brine mixture.

homestyle-hot-cocoa.name: Homestyle Hot Cocoa
instructions: Whisk together sugar, cocoa powder, cinnamon, and salt in a saucepan; stir in boiling water. Whisk until sugar is dissolved.

Bring cocoa mixture to a simmer over medium-high heat, stirring constantly, about 2 minutes; stir milk and 1/2 cup half-and-half into water mixture. Cook and stir just until hot, about 2 minutes. Remove saucepan from heat; stir in remaining half-and-half and vanilla extract.
Divide cocoa into mugs, top with marshmallows, and serve.

honey-wheat-bread-i.name: Honey Wheat Bread I
instructions: Dissolve yeast and sugar in 1/2 cup warm water.

Combine milk, 1/4 cup water, shortening, honey, salt and wheat flour in food processor or bowl. Mix in yeast mixture, and let rest 15 minutes. Add bread flour, and process until dough forms a ball. Knead dough by processing an additional 80 seconds in food processor, or mix and knead by hand 10 minutes. Place the dough in a buttered bowl, and turn to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap. Let dough rise for 45 minutes, or until almost doubled.

Punch down, and divide dough in half. Roll out each half, and pound out the bubbles. Form into loaves, and place in buttered 9x5 inch bread pans. Butter the tops of the dough, and cover loosely with plastic wrap. Let rise in a warm area until doubled; second rise should take about 30 minutes.
Place a small pan of water on the bottom shelf of the oven. Preheat oven to 191°C (190 degrees C).
Bake for 25 to 35 minutes, or until tops are dark golden brown. Butter crusts while warm. Slice when cool.

honey-garlic-shrimp-chow-mein.name: Honey-garlic Shrimp Chow Mein

ingredients.prep: hong kong style pan fried noodles, par cooked according to package instructions

instructions: In a 2-cup (480ml) liquid measuring cup, combine the ginger, garlic, soy sauce, and honey.

Pour about ⅓ cup (80 ml) of sauce over the shrimp in a medium bowl and toss to coat. Reserve the rest of the sauce. Cover the shrimp with plastic wrap and marinate in the refrigerator for 30 minutes to 1 hour.

In a wok or deep skillet over medium-high heat, heat 2 tablespoons of cooking oil, then add the par-cooked noodles. Cook for 1-2 minutes, allowing the noodles to crisp, then flip and cook for another 1-2 minutes. Transfer to a plate and set aside.
In the same wok, heat 1 tablespoon of cooking oil, then add the onion, broccoli, carrots, and ⅓ cup (80 ml) of the sauce. Cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the onions are translucent.
Add the cabbage and season with salt and pepper to taste. Cook for 2 minutes more, or until the vegetables have softened.

Push the vegetables to one side of the wok and add the remaining cooking oil to the empty side, followed by the marinated shrimp. Cook for 1 minute without disturbing, then flip the shrimp over and cook for another 2 minutes, until the shrimp are pink and their tails have curled in.

Mix the cooked shrimp with the vegetables and add the crispy chow mein noodles and the remaining sauce. Toss gently and cook for another 2-3 minutes, or until everything is well combined. Garnish with sliced scallions.
Enjoy!

honeydew-with-salami-and-pistachios.name: Honeydew With Salami And Pistachios

In a bowl, mix olive oil, lemon juice, minced garlic, salt and pepper. Put the sardines on a plate and put them with the mixture and leave them to be marinated for 15-20 minutes in the refrigerator.

Heat the grill or grill with medium-high heat. If you use a coal grill, wait till the pits are well lit. If you wear an electrical or gas grill, make sure it's hot and hot before you put the sardines.

Put the sardines directly onto the hot grill. Cooking for 3-4 minutes on each side, depending on the size of the sardines, or until the skin is crispy and golden and the flesh feels tender to touch. Avoid them being moved too far so they don't get broken up.

how-to-make-baked-suckling-pig-step-by-s.name: How To Make Baked Suckling Pig Step By Step
how-to-make-baked-suckling-pig-step-by-s.original_name: Cómo hacer cochinillo al horno paso a paso

ingredients.item: 2 cucharadas soperas de manteca de cerdo

instructions: To start with this recipe, the tracksuit should be complete but well clear. Ask your supplier or butcher to give it to you completely clean, to save you all this work at home. That includes that they remove the cheeses or intestines from the animal and cut them throughout but only under them.

Building from this point, we're going to pat the flesh of the cochinillo with the end of a fork. We will do this widely, here and there, about its whole surface. The aim is to allow heat to escape while cooking. Otherwise, she would get trapped and create bubbles in the pork's skin.
Optional, you can also make some surface cuts to your skin using the tip of a sharp knife.
Then we'll put the meat aside for a while, and we'll have to have a snack. But first, we'll put the oven up to 180 degrees Celsius.
If you really want to do the most traditional frat, you're gonna need a mortar. That's where we're gonna put our garlic teeth after we peel them and cut their tipples.
We will also add a touch of fat salt to taste and 4 or 5 grams of pepper (fresh, preferably, giving more flavour to the condiment).
Finally, we'll wash up the parsley well and scold and clear his leaves and put them in his mortar.
Then we'll shredder the whole mixture till we get a more or less homogenous dough.
Enseguida, colocaremos la manteca en el mortero, y volveremos a mezclar todo.
With this condiment we will put together an oven-suitable fountain as well as the whole surface of the cochinillo.

Then it will be time to put the tracksuit at the fountain. Traditionally, the animal is never put directly onto the bottom of the container, so we have several options to manage this: first we get several branches of rosemary and cover with them the bottom of the source, and then place the pigeon onto them. Also, this will give a delicious perfume to meat. Another, more rudimentary, option is to moist two stick spoons and place them at the bottom of the fountain so that the weight of the cochinillo will be distributed onto them.
Finally, the most modern choice is simply to replace the source with an oven grid. A lower oven plate should be put, however, to recover the juices that can be released while baking.

To make sure that the cochineal doesn't resist while cooking, I usually have to come with the baking with some water, what's to be put at the bottom of the baking fountain or at the plate under the grid. I mix 150 milliliter of Red Wine (can also be used with white) with 200 milliliter of water. There are cooks who prefer to use only water. Finally, the goal is to keep the pigeon hydrated but the wine will also give you a touch of taste.
Finalmente, vamos a colocar las hojas de laurel en la mezcla de agua y vino.

Then we'll get the cochineal into the oven, and once I'm inside, we'll put the temperature down to 150 degrees. We have to be sure to put the fountain right into the oven centre and that it emits heat up and down.

We'll let the stroller cook for an hour and a half. We will often get back to the oven to check that the liquid with which the meat will be hydrated isn't run out. That vapour atmosphere should be kept constantly in the oven, and it's one of the keys to success of our recipe. To sum up, if water's running out, we have to add more. When you add them, let them slide first onto the skin of the cochinillo, to keep them well hydrated.
Once the time's up, we'll get that frat out of the oven to turn it around. The easiest thing to do with a clear rag. Be careful that you don't get burned with this.
Optional, you can put a little more butter and put them back into the oven.

We'll get the oven's temperature up to 180 degrees and let the cochinillo get baked for an hour and a quarter. Just as we have before, we will often check that the pigeon doesn't run out of fluids and if we are to add some more, we will have a bath with the meat before it falls to the bottom of the plate.

Once this time's up, we'll give the frat a hot blow. That's what will make his skin crisp and toast. We're simply gonna get our temperature up to 200 degrees and let the cochinillo cook for about a last 15 minutes. For this stage of the recipe, the ideal thing is to leave up the part we want toasted. Then we'll have to turn the pigeon back.
That's it. Once this cooking time's up, the tracksuit will be ready to eat. Enjoy!

how-to-make-banana-cookies-comedera.name: How To Make Banana Cookies
instructions: It purifies the oven to 180 been 3 been (177 been 3).
Forra una bandeja apta para horno con papel para hornear.

In another bowl, place mature pawned bananas and put them with a fork and get a soft purée. If you prefer some small pieces of banana in the cookies, leave them thicker.
Add the banana purée to the butter and egg mixture and bat until all ingredients are well combined.
Tamiza la harina y la levadura sobre la masa principal y mezcla suavemente hasta que no queden grumos de harina.
Add cinnamon powder and minced nuts (if you are using them) and mix again until they are evenly distributed into the mass.
With the help of a spoiler, put portions of dough onto the plate, leaving about 5 cm of space between them as the cookies will expand during cooking.
It forms circles with mass and clamps them slightly with the back of a spoon or with his fingers.
Wet the cookies in the pressurized oven for about 15- 18 minutes, or until they are golden at the edges.
Once baked, remove the plate from the oven and let the cookies cool onto the plate for a few minutes before transferring them to a grid to completely cool them.

how-to-make-piquillo-peppers-stuffed-wit.name: How To Make Piquillo Peppers Stuffed With Cod
how-to-make-piquillo-peppers-stuffed-wit.original_name: Cómo hacer pimientos de piquillo rellenos de bacalao

ingredients.item: ¼ cup of ground bread or a large tablespoon of wheat flour
Un chorro de vino blanco seco

instructions: To start with this recipe, we will start with the fact that cod is already deselined (for those who do not use fresh fish). The fish should be clear and skin free and completely free from thorns or other parts that can interfere with the smooth texture of fish meat, which will be the basis of our picket pilling.

Lo siguiente que haremos es desmigajar el bacalao. Luego, calentaremos agua en una olla, y herviremos el pescado. Una vez que el agua rompa a hervor, apagaremos el fuego y retiraremos la olla. Entonces escurriremos el pescado con ayuda de un colador.
At this point, we will preheat the oven at 180 degrees Celsius.
To continue, we will reserve the fish and care about the onion. Let's peel and get her into small pieces. We will also do the same with garlic: peel your teeth and mince them very finely.

We'll heat up a pint with a good jet of oil at medium heat. That's where we'll cook the pieces of onion and garlic, with care to remove them often. We'll have to wait till the onion starts to become slightly clear and the garlic starts to get some golden nuances.

Reaching this point, we will add flour or frosted bread. We'll cook a few minutes and add the wine jet. We'll mix well and give another couple of minutes of cooking so that the alcohol will be reduced a little.
Then we will add the frosted cod, which should be well drained and dry. We'll help ourselves with a kitchen or spoon spatula to undo the bigger pieces that could be left.

At this point, we will add a touch of pepper to cod and we will also add salt exclusively where fresh fish are used. We will avoid this ingredient when using deselated fish, because there are always enough salt residues to give the plate a taste. Then we'll mix well.
Cocinaremos la mezcla, revolviendo con frecuencia, solo hasta que el pescado cambie de color.
Later we will pass the mixture through a blender or we will crush it enough to get a thick dough (if you prefer a pilling with pieces, you could skip this step).
Now we'll have our picket peppers and we'll fill them very delicately with the preparations we have come to prepare. As we fill them up, we'll put them up at a plate or oven plate.
Luego, con la ayuda de una brocha los recubriremos muy ligeramente de aceite, y los meteremos al horno durante veinte minutos.
After this time, we'll get our peppers out of the oven and they'll be ready to eat.

Now, you'll add that sodith to the porocas next to the clot and the rest of the frying basil leaves.

how-to-make-strawberries-with-chocolate-.name: How To Make Strawberries With Chocolate ( Recipe)

ingredients.item: Fondant chocolate for melting (you can buy here)
Candy melts from white chocolate to melt (you can buy here)
Candy melts de chocolate rosa (puedes comprar

instructions: We wash strawberries in water We put chocolate into a bowl We melt chocolate or Candy Melts to the bathroom Mary or to the microwave, we remove every few seconds and stir well (if you use cover chocolate and you want a professional result, you can temperate chocolate) We dip strawberries into chocolate leave the top of the slice free to hold them Let them run onto the bowl We place them on a plate with oven paper Let them cool till the chocolate solidifies

We repeat the process with the white and the color chocolate We melt the white Candy Melt (you can buy here) and we bath the strawberries and then place them on a plate with scroll paper. We let them dry. We do the same with pink Candy Melt (you can buy here)

Once solidified chocolate comes time to decorate them To decorate strawberries we melt chocolate into the bathroom Mary or microwave and put a disposable pastry sleeve into a high glass. We put the chocolate inside and cut the tip to get it out very finite Another easier option we propose to you is to use a Silicone Decopen as this one, with several nozzles and that will serve you for whatever décor you have to do with chocolate or any cream.

Put a tablespoon of cheeses at the bottom of each tomato. Break an egg and place it carefully inside the tomato onto the mixture. If the tomato is small and the egg doesn't suit complete, remove some of the clear.

hummingbird-cupcakes.name: Hummingbird Cupcakes
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease and flour 3 regular cupcake pans.

Combine flour, sugar, salt, baking soda, and cinnamon in a large bowl. Add eggs and oil, stirring until dry ingredients are moistened. Stir in bananas, pineapple, pecans, and vanilla extract. Pour batter into the pans, filling cups about 1/2 full.

Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into the center of cupcake comes out clean, about 25 minutes. Transfer pans to a wire rack and cool for 10 minutes. Remove cupcakes from pans and let cool completely, at least 20 minutes.

In the meantime, beat cream cheese and butter together using an electric mixer until creamy. Add powdered sugar gradually, beating until light and fluffy. Stir in vanilla extract. Frost the cooled cupcakes.

hyderabad-dum-biryani.name: Hyderabad Dum Biryani
instructions: Place black peppercorns, cloves, cardamom, cinnamon sticks, star anise, and kala jeera into a spice grinder; grind to a fine powder.
Place cilantro and mint leaves in the bowl of a food processor; pulse until coarsely chopped.

Combine spice powder, cilantro-mint mixture, yogurt, lemon juice, ginger-garlic paste, chili powder, biryani masala powder, and turmeric in a large glass or ceramic bowl. Add chicken; toss to evenly coat. Cover bowl with plastic wrap and marinate in the refrigerator, about 2 hours.
Bring water and rice to a boil in a saucepan; add 2 bay leaves. Reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and simmer until rice is partially cooked through and still firm, about 5 minutes. Drain; set aside.
Combine milk and saffron in a small bowl; stir and set aside.
Heat ghee in a large pot with a tight-fitting lid over medium-high heat. Add onions; cook and stir until golden brown, about 15 minutes. Drain on paper towels; set aside.
Reduce heat to low. Add remaining 2 bay leaves and chile peppers; cook and stir until fragrant, 1 to 2 minutes. Carefully transfer 1 tablespoon ghee from the pot to a small bowl; set aside.

Remove chicken from marinade, wiping excess marinade off; add chicken to the pot. Discard remaining marinade. Cook over medium heat until no longer pink, about 2 minutes per side; spread rice on top, then sprinkle on onions. Drizzle reserved ghee and saffron milk over rice and onions.

Cover the pot; cook over high heat, about 8 minutes. Reduce heat to low; continue cooking, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat; let stand, covered, until rice is tender and an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of chicken reads 74°C (74 degrees C), about 15 minutes more.

11-herbs-and-spices-fried-chicken.name: 11 Herbs And Spices Fried Chicken
instructions: Beat the eggs into the buttermilk.
Add the chicken pieces to the buttermilk mixture and chill for an hour.
In a large mixing bowl, mix the flour with all the herbs and spices.
Coat the soaked chicken pieces in the herbed flour mixture, shaking off any excess.
Heat oil in a large sauce pan to 325˚F (170°C), then turn to low heat.
Fry the chicken in batches for 15 minutes until the chicken is cooked and the skins turn golden brown.
Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate.
Enjoy!
